operations.value: Oral magnesium commonly causes dose-related gastrointestinal effects such as loose stools, cramping, or diarrhea; glycinate may be marketed as gentler, but comparative human tolerability evidence is limited and product elemental-magnesium content still determines exposure.
operations.notes: Uses oral-magnesium safety evidence while explicitly avoiding a strong comparative tolerability claim for glycinate.

sources.title: Electrolyte disorders with platinum-based chemotherapy: mechanisms, manifestations and management
Magnesium Bisglycinate Supplementation in Healthy Adults Reporting Poor Sleep: A Randomized, Placebo-Controlled Trial

notes: Adds a practical gastrointestinal tolerability warning and elemental-magnesium context.
